INFORMATION FROM DAMAGE SURVEY INDICATES AN EF0 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED WIND SPEED OF 85 MPH BEGAN 3 AND HALF MILES EAST NORTHEAST OF MONTPELIER NEAR GREEN LANE AND TRACKED NORTHEAST ENDING NEAR PARISH ROAD 159 JACKSON LANE. DAMAGE PATH WAS 1500 FEET WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 50 YARDS. A SINGLE WIDE MOBILE HOME SHIFTED OFF ITS BLOCK PIERS AND LOSS PART OF THE METAL ROOF. A METAL SHED WAS DESTROYED AND A FEW TREES WERE SNAPPED ABOVE THE BASE.
TRAILER RECEIVED MODERATE DAMAGE AND WAS ROLLED OVER. IN ADDITION MULTIPLE TREES WERE DAMAGED.
POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE ON HIGHWAY 405 IN WHITE CASTLE. POWER POLES DOWNED AND ROOF DAMAGE.
PRELIMINARY HIGH END EF-2 DAMAGE. MORE DETAILS WILL BE PROVIDED UPON THE CONCLUSION OF THE SURVEY.
TORNADO CONFIRMED BY RADAR TDS. EMERGENCY MANAGER RECEIVED MULTIPLE REPORTS OF TREE AND STRUCTURE DAMAGE AND IS INVESTIGATING.
CAMP MOVED 6 INCHES, MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO A FEW STRUCTURES, POWERLINES AND POLE DOWN

*** 2 FATAL *** THE MAXIMUM WIND AND EF SCALE FOR ASSUMPTION PARISH TO ST JAMES PARISH STRONG TORNADO HAS BEEN INCREASED SLIGHTLY TO LOWER END EF3 TORNADO...VERSUS PREVIOUS UPPER EF2 SCALE RATING...WITH MAXIMUM WIND OF 140 MPH BASED ON EVALUATION OF ADDITIONAL DATA. PATH LENGTH OF 21 MILES AND PATH WIDTH OF 300-350 YD REMAINS THE SAME. PUBLIC INFORMATION STATEMENT WILL BE RELEASED THIS EVENING DESCRIBING THE TORNADOES IN SOUTHEAST LOUISIANA AND SOUTH MISSISSIPPI.

REPORT OF TORNADO CROSSING MS RIVER AT RIVER MILE 164 NEAR ROMEVILLE CAUSING A BREAKAWAY OF 45 BARGES. MARINE ASSETS STAGED IN THE AREA RESPONDED QUICKLY TO SECURE THE SITUATION. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED SEVERAL POWERLINES DOWNED IN CONVENT.

*** 2 FATAL *** UPDATED INFORMATION FROM DAMAGE SURVEY INDICATES THAT AN UPPER END EF2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ABOUT 3 MILE WEST OF PLATTENVILLE AND TRACKED NORTHEAST FOR APPOXIMATELY 20MILES ENDING AT AIRLINE HIGHWAY ABOUT 7 MILES NORTHEAST OF CONVENT. DAMAGE PATH WAS 21 MILES. A WIDE DAMAGE PATH WAS NOTED...UP TO 300 YARDS WIDE IN SOME AREAS. EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO HOUSES AND BUSINESS NOTED IN PAINCOURTVILLE AREA. AN RV PARK WAS HEAVILY DAMAGED ON HIGHWAY 44 NORTHWEST OF CONVENT WITH TWO FATALITIES
PRELIMINARY REPORT FROM SURVEY IS A VERY WIDE TRACK IN CONVENT. MORE DETAILS WILL FOLLOW UPON THE COMPLETION OF THE TRACK SURVEY.
TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT THE SUGAR HILL RV PARK NW OF CONVENT. NUMBER OF INJURIES AND A POSSIBLE FATALITY. EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT AND FIRST RESPONDERS ON SCENE

INFORMATION FROM DAMAGE SURVEY INDICATES AN EF1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED WIND SPEED OF 105 MPH BEGAN NEAR DONLEY BURKS ROAD WEST OF HIGHWAY 43 AND TRACKED NORTHEAST ENDING AROUND OSBORN MOODY ROAD SOUTHWEST POPLARVILLE. DAMAGE PATH WAS 9.7 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 150 YARDS. MINOR TO MODERATE DAMAGE OCCURRED TO A FEW HOMES AND NUMEROUS TREES DOWNED AND UPROOTED IN RURAL WEST PEARL RIVER COUNTY.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND TREES SNAPPED AND UPROOTED NEAR MILE MARKER 12 ON INTERSTATE 55. WINDS ESTIMATED AT 100 MPH RATING THE TORNADO AN EF1. WIDTH OF 200 YARDS AND A PATH LENGTH OF APPROXIMATELY ONE MILE. COULD NOT OBSERVE IF DAMAGE EXTENDED FURTHER DUE TO LACK OF ACCESS.

A WEAK TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN SOUTHEAST OF GREENSBURG UPROOTING SEVERAL LARGE TREES AND SNAPPING LARGE BRANCHES AND SMALL TREE TRUNKS. MAXIMUM WIND SPEED ESTIMATED AT 95 MPH WITH EF1 SCALE RATING. PATH LENGTH 0.20 MILES AND PATH WIDTH 50 YARDS. TIME OF EVENT ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

A WEAK TORNADO TRACK STARTED ABOUT 5 MILES SOUHTWEST OF THE COMMUNITY OF TANGIPAHOA. THE TORNADO CROSSED INTERSTATE HIGHWAY 55 DOWNING OR SNAPPING SEVERAL DOZEN PINE TREES. TRAFFIC WAS STOPPED FOR A SHORT PERIOD TO CLEAR THE DEBRIS FROM THE ROADWAY. THE TORNADO CONTINUED TO TRAVEL NORTHEAST AND CROSSED HIGHWAY 51 THEN WEAKENED CONSIDERABLY AND DISSIPATED NEAR HIGHWAY 1057 ABOUT 1.5 MILES EAST OF TANGIPAHOA. MAXIMUM WIND 95 MPH WITH AN EF1 RATING. PATH LENGTH 4 MILES AND MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH 250 YD
TREES DOWNED AND DEBRIS BLOWN ACROSS INTERSTATE 55 NEAR MILE MARKER 56.
